description: Budgets for the Playwright perf suite. Soft budgets are log-oriented guardrails. Hard budgets fail the spec when exceeded.
notes: These thresholds are intended for the local Windows Electron test environment. CI noise can differ by runner.
Use docs/developer/quality/baselines/perf-*.json diffs to inspect slower regressions in detail.

Bundle chunk-level enforcement still lives in docs/developer/quality/baselines/bundle-budget.json plus scripts/check-bundle-budget.mjs. The E2E perf spec only performs a lightweight main bundle assertion.

canvasOpen.note: Measured from the PSD import command to the first editable canvas-ready state. The ready state requires a loaded project, visible layer list, and a live canvas element with non-zero dimensions. Internal probes also track project-ready, layer-list-ready, and editable-canvas-ready milestones so DOM-visible envelope drift can be separated from app-side regressions.

editorInteraction.note: Measured on a warm editor after importing the test PSD. Each sample resets to select mode, selects a representative ViviMesh, enters mesh edit, then exits back to select mode. The coarse browser-visible envelope is still recorded for diagnostics, but the enforced regression signal should come from the internal app probes for layer-selection, tool-switch, and mesh-overlay readiness.

fps.note: Measured from requestAnimationFrame cadence in the Electron renderer. This is a compositor-vsync proxy rather than a direct PixiJS ticker measurement, but it is stable enough for regression detection.

bundle.note: Strict chunk budgets are enforced by docs/developer/quality/baselines/bundle-budget.json plus scripts/check-bundle-budget.mjs. The E2E perf spec keeps a lighter main-bundle soft/hard guard around the current baseline.
